description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

🔄 Content Updated (Published: June 15, 2026)

A disaster certificate is issued to confirm the degree of damage when a home or other property is damaged by natural events such as earthquakes, heavy rain, or snow. This certificate is needed to file insurance claims and apply for various relief measures.

About Issuing Disaster Certificates (Building Damage)

If you need a disaster certificate, fill out the required form, affix your seal, and submit it to the Disaster Prevention and Crisis Management Division along with printed photos showing the damage.

Certificates will be issued in order, starting from locations where city staff have confirmed the damage.

Disaster certificates are issued based on the situation approximately one month after the disaster occurs.

Disaster Certificate Form (PDF file: 32.6KB)

Disaster Certificate Form (Excel file: 25.5KB)

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/emergency/13706.html
summary: ℹ️ Disaster Certificates for Hail Damage from June 12
